notable_comments.text: I've been running a copywriting agency for ~5 years now and saw this play out in real time - there's always gonna be arbitrage between people who know how to recognise good copy and people who don't. My team switched from hiring Upwork copywriters to using Copywhiz AI for about 80% of our client work, and honestly, the AI output converts better for routine stuff. But the thing is - we still need humans with expertise to evaluate what's good and to handle the high-concept creative work.
notable_comments.author: @UseCopywhizAI

notable_comments.text: I create grand slam $100M offers and sales copies for different types of businesses on Fiverr using AI. Still, the irony is that local business owners don't understand the value of copy written with AI. And that's right they can't judge if it is a good copy or not. and give bad reviews saying the writer is using AI. Meanwhile, online business owners are totally the opposite.
notable_comments.author: @hashamshafqat

notable_comments.text: At best AI will be like a hammer. It's not just gonna build an entire house for you. Until I see it, I truly don't buy into any notion that AI will take over.
notable_comments.author: @highsol222

notable_comments.text: Copywriting is too much of a creative process to be replaced by AI. AI is good in math, logic in something has determined rules and boundaries, it's good in non-divergent thinking. And you need the complete opposite for copywriting. TLDR - AI influence in copywriting is overblown. At best it's going to be a tool in a copywriter's toolbox.
notable_comments.author: @SuperLari1234

notable_comments.text: AI Sounds robotic always when i try to use it for some Sabri Subi Mails level of copywriting ... & i only reached his style when i tried to talk about my own experiences, thoughts going trough those hardships that i created a product to solve those exact problems i faced .. AI will never beat human creativity in my opinion ... even content writers are now Content Editors of AI Content

notable_comments.text: As someone at the forefront of generative AI, I've heard this argument from many that money won't matter in the future (it's not an original Hormozi thought). And I completely disagree. The nature of humans will always be to compete against other humans. As a result, there will always be a human element to complement AI that will provide a competitive advantage

notable_comments.text: This is fantastic. The only issue I have is a lot of clients want human content because they want to know the freelancer actually did some work and didn't just use AI. Also, human content ranks higher on Google. Can you create a video on how you transform AI content to bypass AI detection software. Personally, I find this takes a lot of time, and because you're constantly moving words around the quality also tends to suffer.
